transcript.text: How's it going?
Good, good.
This meeting is being recorded.
Oh, good.
Great to hear.
Yeah, it's been pretty crazy over here.
A lot of sales calls and not as much capacity.
So trying to juggle that on a daily basis.
See, that's why you need GetAccept.
Makes it so much easier.
Exactly.
Oh, yeah.
Tell me a little bit more about that.
Well, first of all, obviously, we help you close your deals quicker by centralizing all of your communication into one room where everybody has access to all different sales activities.

Obviously, making sure that enablement by decision maker and champion are all aligned with your internal team, making sure that everybody is transparent and up to date on what is happening within the deal cycle, allowing both champion and internally to accelerate the deal volume throughout the internal organizations.
So when I bring someone into like a GetAccept sales room, do they just access it by a link?
And that's...
that's...
that's...
Pretty much how they get in.
Correct.
They come in through a link that will be available to them the entire time that the data is live.
All of the documentation, all of the conversations, everything is aligned within the room so everybody can come back and there's never a confusion on that for your side.
We now have AI to enable summaries of all the conversations with to-do points so it's easy for you to follow up and basically automatically create proposal templates, email outreach, etc., etc.

So it is super easy for you to manage, particularly when you're in a high-volume account, to manage several different conversations at same time and let AI and the room itself take care of most of the work for you.
I mean, I did tell you both on that first call that I would love a tool like that here at GrowthX, so it makes a lot of sense.

I guess, like, one of the big differences would be, like, if I curate a Notion page, I'm doing a lot of the work, but with GetAccept, it's going to automatically generate a lot of the context from it.
Conversations that I've had.
Of course, exactly.
And also bring like the prospect into that sales room so that, you know, it's like they're a part, they're an active part of the sales conversation as information evolves.
Like, do they have the ability to like comment in the sales room?
Sorry, I'm just asking too many questions about.
I'm more than happy to set you up with a demo with one of our guys.
It's, mean, I've run sales organizations before and I never came across a tool like this.
And it just basically is mind-blowing once you realize the potential, what you can do with it.
Everybody basically, you know, invests in top of the funnel kind of things like, you a gong or a clay to basically get the data in.
But nobody focuses on the actual improvement of the sales processes, both internally and as a customer experience point of view.
So, yeah, no, it's completely highly customizable.
All the templates are customizable to you.
So, it's basically almost like a white label.
You have full visibility and all

It automatically integrates within your chosen CRM system, so all your fields will always be optimized based on the conversations that you have and the updates that come out of the room will directly be pushed back into your CRM system.

You can connect or integrate things like a gong, for example, or a sales loft, cetera, kind of like more a lead magnet-specific kind of tool in there, so that automates it automatically, so all of the information is there.
It basically eradicates 50%, 60% of your admin overnight by basically using a tool like this.

It's so interesting because the last company I worked for, Seros, they had like a platform for graphic designers to create interactive content, but they also had a product where non-designers could scale out the templates.
Their hypothesis was like, if the content's more visual, you can get people to spend more time with it, right?
If it's interactive, then they're going to actually explore as opposed to, you you send a document.
It's got a bunch of information.
But what's interesting is like at Seros, we would do everything we could to allow the content to help us stand out in the sales process.
But here at GrowthX, the problem that we're solving, it's so significant that as valuable as GetAccept would be, and I'm sure it would help, right?
It would give prospects a better sales experience.
GrowthX is like selling itself.
To be honest.
And, you know, it's just interesting.
Like as a salesperson, you can do so much.
But at the end of the day, if the problem you're solving isn't significant enough, it's like, you know, no tool is going to help you close more deals.
It's kind of my hypothesis.
Now, with that said, if it's a competitive deal cycle, something like your product would absolutely help you get over the line.

Yeah, and I think obviously identifying the pain solution element of that as quick as possible gives the client the aha moment early on in the conversations, making sure that every conversation is tailored and personalized.

I think these are things nowadays make the difference when we hear from our existing customers from the first conversation we've had to until them actually them integrating and using their tool, it makes a huge difference in the way that they operate and the customer service that they deliver.

So their prospects and their customers are saying the same thing, like it's an amazing experience, like the whole sales flow and everything else around it just becomes more enjoyable for everybody involved.
Makes a ton of sense.
The first impression and how you continue on it like is a make or break in the deal cycle.
Cool.
Well, excited to chat with you both today.
Wanted to kind of quickly recap the first conversation, align on use cases, and then talk a little bit about the opportunity against the competitors that you mentioned, PandaDoc.
Tick, Trumpet, and Align.
Also can use the time to just answer any outstanding questions that you all have, given that our timeline is like four to six weeks out right now.
So don't want to put you in a position where you feel rushed, but do just want to be transparent that things are moving really fast here.
So, you know, what you mentioned to me, Bastiaan and Calle, the content manager just left.
You guys are building a category of the digital sales room, a lot more MQLs coming through in AI search.
But organic traffic has been pretty much flat for the last one to two years, around 10 to 15,000 monthly organic visits.
And so there are two use cases that we talked about being pretty impactful here.
The first was educational content, helping prospects in your ICP understand what digital sales rooms are, why they matter, how they can transform the sales process.
But also, you know, thinking in terms of like...
Like thought leadership content, the million-dollar AE, anything you want to add to that summary, Bastiaan?
I think besides the educational side of things, that's obviously the top end of the funnel.
I think the part that we're also really focusing on is driving that educational buyer into more of a consideration, kind of like final stage.
And then segmenting that by buyer type or segment.
So an enterprise versus a mid-market versus, because everybody has a different pain point that they're trying to solve, depending on the size of the business.
I think it's difficult to, at the moment, to funnel them precisely because they all behave and they all look for the same, I guess, type of content.
It's very top level.

And for them to realize what they are and what their pain points are, I think from a content perspective, that requires a bit of a deeper, like a deep dive slash intelligent approach to really make that stick.
I think we have good content, like some of our most successful content.
Those are very generic.
What are the top qualities of a sales manager, for example, is one of our best-performing posts.
It has relevance to what we do, obviously, but it's not relevant to the actual sales cycle part of the business.

So we could either churn out loads of these articles, something that you see that Clay has done particularly on their address book kind of things, like where the headquarters from certain businesses, a lot of people will search on that kind of stuff and will get to their site.
There is an overlay in terms of interest, but it's not really the sales interest that you'll be looking for.
It just generates a lot of awareness.

Now, we want to move away from that awareness part and more into a consideration part where we're catching people that are actually understanding their pain points and their problems and proactively looking for a solution to that.
And not going down the route specifically of comparisons, as in like, oh, that already made that decision.
Is a panda dog better or is there a price difference?
And it's more like that layer that sits above that, so that literally that consideration phase, as in I have an issue with my sales pipe.
Who can help me with solving that, which is us.
Absolutely.
And I love what you said about, you know, you can create even more content by segmenting SMB versus mid-market versus enterprise, or even just mid-market versus enterprise alone.
So it makes a ton of sense there.
Have you guys done a lot of comparison content between Trumpet, Aligned, Panditop?
There are some out there, particularly within our PPC campaigns that are focusing on that.
So we have competitor comparison pages, et cetera.
But they're, as usual, they're very top-level kind of things, which basically ends up in us being predominantly the more sophisticated choice in terms of the product features usage.
But it comes down to a, I would say, a combination of pricing.
Like we are going to be a bit more expensive because we're more an all-in-one platform.
Versus the one product kind of like vendor obviously has a bit of more of a credibility.
When it comes to their expertise, because they only have one specific feature, while we are, you know, stretched across four or five different product features that people can use.
Still good quality, but people will evaluate that on the basis of the whole platform set of features rather than just the core feature that we have.
Makes sense.

And if folks are, you know, exploring, like, solving their pain points around generating pipeline or, you know, their closed win rates, and they're connecting it to content that your brand's creating, when they get to some of those comparison pages, they're already thinking in terms of, like, you guys as the thought leader, right?
As opposed to maybe they land on that comparison page, and it just becomes a matter of, well, what's the price of this tool versus that tool?
Looking into SEMrush here, you know, one thing that stood out is, you guys are doing a great job, as is, if we're comparing against Trumpet, and we're comparing against Align in particular.
Now,
PandaDoc, it's a broader and even tangential solution set, so it makes sense that there's a 27x difference between their traffic and your traffic.

But I think some of the opportunities, obviously, being able to widen the gap between Trumpet and Align, so targeting keywords that Align ranks for, thinking in terms of increasing the content velocity, because you already have that head start.
And these are just like some pieces, I think, that stood out, you know, immediately when looking at you guys' SEMrush page.
And any thoughts from, you know, either of you around just insights that you've had between GetAccept and some of the competitors you mentioned?
So, one thing I haven't noticed, or have noticed is, I mean,

I mean, these competitors are strong in different channels, Even though they're not necessarily 100% one-to-one competition, they're really strong in search and more traditional channels, whereas Trumpet and Aligned are probably stronger than us on social, organic social in particular.

So if there's a way for us to get additional sort of content benefits or audience insights benefits out of this type of work, that we could also apply in that channels to get like a whole content machine going, I think that would be very, very good for us.
Aside from furthering our current advantage, as you were saying, Tyler.
That makes sense.
And what we would do for you guys, we would focus on editorial.
And if there's like a programmatic play, we would definitely want to take that into account.
During the strategy sprint.

But in terms of the way that we would work with you guys to create content, you know, we go audience first, as we talked a lot about in the first call, thinking in terms of who are those personas, right?
And what are all the topics that you want to be the expert around?
And so once we get into, you know, the engagement and develop your content OS, I wanted to give you a look at Engine's content OS here.
This is just like their net new content that we've posted in the strategy sprint for them.
But we've also done a lot of content for refreshes.
So if there's any content that's over a year old that you're starting to see the performance drop, this can also factor into what we do for you during phase one and phase two of the engagement.
And so what we're doing here is we're really mapping out the topics that Engine wants to create content around.
And so these would be topics similar to, you know, what we've mentioned about different stages of this.
Sales process, right?
Or common pain points that account executives face in the sales cycle.
The digital sales room itself could be one of these topics that we're creating content around.
And what we're doing within each topic is we're generating an assignment.

And then as we use the workflows to create the content, we are going to collaborate in a Google Doc or in a Slack channel, whatever's easiest for you guys, so that you have the ability to give feedback on the content as it's approved and ready to publish in your CMS.
In these examples, a lot of the work that we do is around the keyword volume, the difficulty.
And then we use that to inform the prioritization of the content that we create.
And so we would create this content OS for you guys.
And then over the next month or two, everything that you're seeing here would actually get moved into the platform.
So right now we deliver reporting around LL.

Well, I'm visibility through scrunch, but as you can see here in our platform, we're starting to develop our own analytics, our own metrics, and then when it comes to the review process, we're actually building this activity feed so that you can start to see different steps in our process, and also so the team has the ability to upload different knowledge-based resources that will inform the workflows directly into the platforms.

So the analytics, know, again, we haven't built that out yet, but this is a little bit around, like, how we would collaborate with you guys and also how we would think about the assignments or the bets that we want to make from a content perspective.
Bastiaan, anything you want me to go in more detail around in terms of the way that we work or how phase one and phase two are set up?
I think here is an interesting one, what's the impact, obviously.
I mean, I'm not interested
Just from a traffic perspective, obviously, I want to see something happening from the bottom line.
How do you measure the impact that the SEO part basically does?
Are you guys looking at the last click approach?

Do you have some sort of multi-threat kind of tracking on when a person hits the SEO side or the content side and goes back into search and goes through a PPC ad, you still can attribute value to that?
Like, how are you guys looking at that to qualify the value that you deliver to a business?
Yeah, absolutely.
So for every client, we'll set up like a looker dashboard and we'll look in terms of the growthx pages, right, versus the other pages.
We'll set up, we'll connect to, you your Google search console as well.
And so in terms of attribution, I believe that we are like piggybacking on what you guys have already maybe stood up and have in place.
But we are thinking about this.
a thing.
guess we have We're
Right, in terms of what's the impact of our content on your overall signups, right?
So we are, like for Augment Code, as an example, their guides, you know, we've tracked that they've 2x the traffic on their blog.
And they wanted us just to focus on a different section of their website so that they could have that direct comparison.
And we've seen that if we've 2x the traffic of their blog using the guides that we've created over the last 90 days, we've created about 90 guides in total here.
And it was attributed to like 300 signups in the last week alone.
So that's like one example of how we're tracking the ROI.
Another example here for Tiro, and I believe there is information directly in this deck.
We 55x, you know, their total clicks from the period of time before they were with GrowthX to the six months.
You can see, you know, the lift on the impression side, but reporting can, you know, be fairly custom.
It's just a matter of connecting to the right tools.
If there's a follow-up that's a little more specific, I can definitely take it back to the team.
Just let me know.
I know I'm, you know, doing my best to answer your question, but may not be fully answering it.

Yeah, because I think what we've tried to move away from is kind of like that traffic generation, because we can obviously write quite generically about sales enablement and the sales team kind of like processes and pain points, but it really needs to connect to what we do.
So our focus has been kind of like product heavy.

So you're looking at e-signature, digital signature, or as a proposal software that we've, particularly on the US side, we've really started building out a content strategy and a cluster approach that really complements that.
So we've seen more traffic coming in based on that, that it all starts to convert because obviously we're getting a more authority within those individuals.
And that's being augmented by what we're doing on our PPC side to basically raise quality score or quality rating within Google that we're an authoritative source on that.
But the question is that that's just one angle.
Can we, you know, 5X that on five angles per market, per topic, basically, to ensure that we have that breadth of impression share across all of these different segments?
Because at the moment, it's with the resources that we have and the output that we have, it's very much like one topic, basically, per country, per quarter that we basically do something with.

Then we measure, as in, do we get it from organic to come in or the limited amount of multi-threading that we can do in terms of the attribution to figure out, is this something that basically makes sense to continue or not?

Now, think that's really the question for us, like, we've seen that we could basically increase traffic, but it didn't deliver anything to the bottom line, particularly from a, from organic conversion perspective.

So we really need to have a clear strategy on, and this is where we come down to that very heavily ICP-focused funneling from a person coming in on a content article that then basically gets retargeted or basically has an ongoing almost kind of like engagement conversation using that ICP-relevant content.
And that's the trick I think that we're missing at the moment.

They come in on a pinpoint solution page, at which point we lose them because we're not good enough yet or we don't have the traction yet to really put out enough content to keep on engaging them and convince them of the authority that we have in the space.
That makes sense.

And I don't think we would like change the way that you guys do attribution or focus on, right, like developing more attribution, but we would give you direct insight into how you're performing in LLMs against specific prompts that you care about.
And that can be obviously like a wide.
So of prompts and our general approach to measurement is going to be what you see on this slide here, right?
So the first thing that we're going to do is make sure that the core vitals of the website are in a good place.
The next thing that we're going to be thinking in terms of is activity, right?
So the number of articles we publish and refresh, and then we're thinking in terms of impressions, clicks.
Then we're starting to move into engagement, conversions, and this is all something that we are going to give you a bunch of insight into.
Naturally, if you cannot connect the content that we're creating to signups, right?
You're not going to want to continue with growthx.
It's obviously a long game, but that's part of the process in terms of how we help with analytics.
I can try to see if there's a client who I can share more specifics on the analytics side around, if that would be helpful.
Yeah, because, I mean, again, just being completely European and transparent and blunt to say, like, we're charging, you're charging 12 grand, obviously.
So far, I can see this is very much of a, obviously, content play that you could, that you help us deliver.
What's the, what's the USP value point, you versus a normal SEO content agency that could do the same thing, or they would pitch the same thing to me for half the price?
Like, what's the difference there in terms of what you deliver versus what a content agency delivers?
Yeah, super valid question.
I think it's the talent, right?
So, like, the folks that would be working on your account, that would be the first piece here.
Let's go back to, so, just having a managing director, managing editor, content manager, and it's the, it's the scale, right?
So, being able to get to daily publishing at the quality that we maintain.
And then, you know, it's, it's also going to be.
The way that we're doing everything for you from end to end.

So SEO, GEO strategy, content creation, execution, staging and publishing in the CMS, monitoring and analyzing the LLM performance and giving you, you know, in the very near future, a platform which you can track all of that performance around.
So happy to go in more detail, though.
And as you guys say, like, it's a 12-month minimum contract.
So you're looking at a quite significant investment here.

Do you guys have a guarantee in terms of uplift, in terms of deliverables, in terms of like what even after 12 months we don't work out, you basically will be like, oh, actually after either X amount of months you start to see this or we'll guarantee you get, you know, X, Y, Z and we'll, if you don't get it, we'll give you a discount or whatever, whatever it is.
Like, do you guys have any, any, you know, finger in the pudding to make.
Sure that there's a commitment from your side besides us basically having a commitment of paying a 12-month contract.
The commitment that we make is really around volume, right?
So there's deliverables built into the statement of work.
In the phase one, you're able to see the deliverables around the artifacts that we create, the roadmap of the content we would be working around.
We'll also create deliverables around the competition, build an artifact to make sure that the content is going to be branded to your voice in addition to the calibration content.
And then in phase two, it's really the team that would be assigned to your account and the average of five publish-ready pieces per week, so the daily publishing that I mentioned.
In terms of success.
Best metrics, we're going to continue to keep you in the loop with how you're performing.
But at this time, right, we have like an overwhelming amount of demand for our services.
And so everything that we're signing, it's 12K at the minimum, and it's 12 months at the minimum for Phase 2.
Part of the way we work, it allows you to opt out by Phase 7, or it allows you to opt out by Week 7 of Phase 1.
And so in Phase 1, we're creating those artifacts to give the workflows the context necessary to be as effective as possible.
But we're also publishing, we would probably publish like 10 articles at least by Week 7.

So that combination, in addition to like the roadmap of the assignments that we would be working on, the way that we're working with you, that's what allows you to make an informed decision about continuing into Phase 2 or, you know, opting out and maybe going with a partner that's at the cost, right, as you mentioned.
Okay.
Ba-da!
Ba Ba
Happy to answer any other questions that you have.
think, you know, right now we would be looking at a kickoff date in mid-October to late October if you guys were moving forward, you know, within the next week.
And, yeah, happy to be helpful in any way I can for you.
Do you guys?
If you would have a more detailed case study with one of the clients that you could share that would be super helpful just to get a better understanding of the impact.
You bet.
You had a question, Tyler?
Do you guys work anything, like with all the SEO work going on, do you do any work on acquiring backlinks and stuff like that as well or not?
Is it just the content production?
Yeah, we have done backlink work for clients, but it wouldn't be a focus of the engagement, right?
We believe on our side that, you know, if we can drive higher intent traffic, then that's going to be, you know, the ultimate outcome that we're working towards.
And for backlinks, we would want to focus on making sure that the backlinks that we're acquiring, you know, they're high quality.
Because at the end of the day, like the number doesn't matter as much, right?
It's more about how many unique domains and like where are the backlinks coming from?
So, you know, it could be a part of the work we're doing, but I'll be honest that it wouldn't be like a main, you know, it wouldn't be the main focus.
Got it.
Awesome.
We'll keep me posted, gentlemen.
I'll definitely look into case studies and provide more detail as I find it.
But just try to let us know in the next week or week or two.
So because we wouldn't want to push your start date out until like November or something like that.
Okay.
No, makes sense.
All righty.
Good to see you again.
Have a great rest of the day.
You too.
Thank you so much.
Cheers.
Ciao.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Follow-up discussion on potential partnership between GrowthX and GetAccept, focusing on content strategy and SEO opportunities.](https://fathom.video/share/91jpFjrAsHctg6UzvLCwN3vfXNSFTy3Q?tab=summary&timestamp=0.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [GetAccept seeks to improve organic traffic (currently 10-15k monthly visits) and move from awareness to consideration-focused content](https://fathom.video/share/91jpFjrAsHctg6UzvLCwN3vfXNSFTy3Q?tab=summary&timestamp=0.0)
  - [GrowthX offers a comprehensive content strategy, creation, and analytics solution, emphasizing daily publishing and LLM performance](https://fathom.video/share/91jpFjrAsHctg6UzvLCwN3vfXNSFTy3Q?tab=summary&timestamp=0.0)
  - [GetAccept expressed interest but concerns about ROI and differentiation from other agencies; requested more detailed case studies](https://fathom.video/share/91jpFjrAsHctg6UzvLCwN3vfXNSFTy3Q?tab=summary&timestamp=0.0)

## Topics

### GetAccept's Current Situation and Goals

  - [Content manager recently left](https://fathom.video/share/91jpFjrAsHctg6UzvLCwN3vfXNSFTy3Q?tab=summary&timestamp=379.0)
  - [Building category of digital sales room](https://fathom.video/share/91jpFjrAsHctg6UzvLCwN3vfXNSFTy3Q?tab=summary&timestamp=385.0)
  - [More MQLs coming through AI search](https://fathom.video/share/91jpFjrAsHctg6UzvLCwN3vfXNSFTy3Q?tab=summary&timestamp=392.0)
  - [Organic traffic flat at 10-15k monthly visits for 1-2 years](https://fathom.video/share/91jpFjrAsHctg6UzvLCwN3vfXNSFTy3Q?tab=summary&timestamp=392.0)
  - [Aim to move from general awareness content to consideration-focused, segmented by buyer type/segment](https://fathom.video/share/91jpFjrAsHctg6UzvLCwN3vfXNSFTy3Q?tab=summary&timestamp=433.0)
  - [Need to improve funneling from content to conversion, especially for ICP](https://fathom.video/share/91jpFjrAsHctg6UzvLCwN3vfXNSFTy3Q?tab=summary&timestamp=541.0)

### GrowthX's Offering and Approach

  - [Focus on educational content and thought leadership](https://fathom.video/share/91jpFjrAsHctg6UzvLCwN3vfXNSFTy3Q?tab=summary&timestamp=405.0)
  - [Create content OS with topic mapping and keyword-informed prioritization](https://fathom.video/share/91jpFjrAsHctg6UzvLCwN3vfXNSFTy3Q?tab=summary&timestamp=782.0)
  - [Daily publishing (avg. 5 pieces/week) with quality maintained](https://fathom.video/share/91jpFjrAsHctg6UzvLCwN3vfXNSFTy3Q?tab=summary&timestamp=1373.0)
  - [End-to-end service: SEO, content strategy, creation, execution, CMS publishing, LLM performance analysis](https://fathom.video/share/91jpFjrAsHctg6UzvLCwN3vfXNSFTy3Q?tab=summary&timestamp=1384.0)
  - [12-month minimum contract at $12k/month](https://fathom.video/share/91jpFjrAsHctg6UzvLCwN3vfXNSFTy3Q?tab=summary&timestamp=1409.0)
  - [Phase 1 (7 weeks) allows opt-out before full commitment](https://fathom.video/share/91jpFjrAsHctg6UzvLCwN3vfXNSFTy3Q?tab=summary&timestamp=1516.0)

### Content Strategy and SEO Insights

  - [Opportunity to widen gap with competitors like Trumpet and Align](https://fathom.video/share/91jpFjrAsHctg6UzvLCwN3vfXNSFTy3Q?tab=summary&timestamp=659.0)
  - [Increase content velocity and target keywords Align ranks for](https://fathom.video/share/91jpFjrAsHctg6UzvLCwN3vfXNSFTy3Q?tab=summary&timestamp=693.0)
  - [Focus on editorial content with potential for programmatic play](https://fathom.video/share/91jpFjrAsHctg6UzvLCwN3vfXNSFTy3Q?tab=summary&timestamp=744.0)
  - [Audience-first approach, mapping topics to personas and pain points](https://fathom.video/share/91jpFjrAsHctg6UzvLCwN3vfXNSFTy3Q?tab=summary&timestamp=782.0)
  - [Analytics dashboard to track performance and ROI](https://fathom.video/share/91jpFjrAsHctg6UzvLCwN3vfXNSFTy3Q?tab=summary&timestamp=900.0)

### Competitor Landscape

  - [PandaDoc has 27x more traffic but broader solution set](https://fathom.video/share/91jpFjrAsHctg6UzvLCwN3vfXNSFTy3Q?tab=summary&timestamp=660.0)
  - [Trumpet and Align stronger on organic social media](https://fathom.video/share/91jpFjrAsHctg6UzvLCwN3vfXNSFTy3Q?tab=summary&timestamp=720.0)
  - [GetAccept more sophisticated but potentially more expensive due to all-in-one platform](https://fathom.video/share/91jpFjrAsHctg6UzvLCwN3vfXNSFTy3Q?tab=summary&timestamp=586.0)

### ROI and Performance Measurement

  - [GrowthX provides insights on LLM performance against specific prompts](https://fathom.video/share/91jpFjrAsHctg6UzvLCwN3vfXNSFTy3Q?tab=summary&timestamp=1448.0)
  - [Measures core website vitals, activity (articles published/refreshed), impressions, clicks, engagement, and conversions](https://fathom.video/share/91jpFjrAsHctg6UzvLCwN3vfXNSFTy3Q?tab=summary&timestamp=1269.0)
  - [Can connect to existing attribution systems](https://fathom.video/share/91jpFjrAsHctg6UzvLCwN3vfXNSFTy3Q?tab=summary&timestamp=1502.0)
  - [Examples: 2x blog traffic leading to 300 signups/week (Augment Code), 55x total clicks increase (Tiro)](https://fathom.video/share/91jpFjrAsHctg6UzvLCwN3vfXNSFTy3Q?tab=summary&timestamp=1035.0)

## Next Steps

  - [GrowthX to provide more detailed case studies showing impact and ROI](https://fathom.video/share/91jpFjrAsHctg6UzvLCwN3vfXNSFTy3Q?tab=summary&timestamp=1595.0)
  - [GetAccept to decide within 1-2 weeks for potential mid-to-late October kickoff](https://fathom.video/share/91jpFjrAsHctg6UzvLCwN3vfXNSFTy3Q?tab=summary&timestamp=1671.0)
  - [If proceeding, GrowthX to develop custom content strategy and begin Phase 1 implementation](https://fathom.video/share/91jpFjrAsHctg6UzvLCwN3vfXNSFTy3Q?tab=summary&timestamp=1529.0)

action_items.description: Find and share detailed case study with GetAccept team showing impact of GrowthX services
Discuss with team and decide on GrowthX engagement within 1-2 weeks; inform Tyler of decision
